cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So the problem is about what date one year ago from today is, given that John is correct about today's date. Let me break this down step by step. The question is asking, what is the date one year ago from today, in the format MM/DD/YYYY? So if today is June 19th, 2019, then one year ago would be June 19th, 2018. But let me double-check that. Wait, but maybe there's a trick here. For example, if today was, say, March 1st, 2019, and you go back one year, it's March 1st, 2018. But what if there's a leap year involved? Wait, but June 19th is not affected by leap years because leap years affect February. So going back a year from June 19th, 2019 would be June 19th, 2018. Let me check if 2019 is a leap year. Wait, 2019 isn't a leap year. But that doesn't matter because we're going back a year, not dealing with February. So the date one year ago would be June 19th, 2018. Looking at the options, option A is 06/19/2018. But wait, option F is 06/18/2018. But why would that be an option? Because Jane thought today was June 18th, so maybe someone might mistakenly subtract a day? But the correct answer is one year ago from today (June 19th, 2019), so that's June 19th, 2018. Let me check if there's any other possible issues. For example, if today is June 19th, 2019, then one year ago is June 19th, 2018. So the answer should be option A, 06/19/2018.
